title: Cumulative global production of plastics
subtitle: Cumulative annual production of polymer resin and fibers.
caption:
Cumulative global production of plastics
Cumulative annual production of polymer resin and fibers.

The area chart tracks the total accumulation of polymer resin and fiber production worldwide from 1950 to 2019, measured in billions of tonnes. The vertical axis spans from 0 to 10 billion tonnes, while the horizontal axis marks decades from the mid-20th century to the present. The data shows a distinct exponential growth pattern, where the rate of accumulation accelerates significantly over time. In the early decades, specifically from 1950 through the 1960s, the total volume remains extremely low, hovering near the zero baseline with barely visible accumulation. By 1970, the cumulative total rises slightly but stays well below the 1 billion tonne mark. The curve begins to steepen in the 1980s, crossing the 1 billion tonne threshold around 1980. By 1990, the cumulative total reaches approximately 2 billion tonnes. The slope increases further in the subsequent decades; the total hits 4 billion tonnes around the year 2000 and reaches 6 billion tonnes by 2010. The final segment of the chart, from 2010 to 2019, displays the steepest incline, with the total rising from 6 billion to just under 10 billion tonnes by the end of the period. The visual representation highlights that the majority of all plastic ever produced was manufactured in the most recent decades shown, with the volume added between 2000 and 2019 exceeding the total accumulated during the entire first half of the timeline.
